003 * contributor license agreements.  See the NOTICE file distributed with
004 * this work for additional information regarding copyright ownership.
005 * The ASF licenses this file to You under the Apache License, Version 2.0
006 * (the "License"); you may not use this file except in compliance with
007 * the License.  You may obtain a copy of the License at
008 *
009 *      http://www.apache.org/licenses/LICENSE-2.0
010 *
011 * Unless required by applicable law or agreed to in writing, software
012 * distributed under the License is distributed on an "AS IS" BASIS,
013 * WITHOUT WARRANTIES OR CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied.
014 * See the License for the specific language governing permissions and
015 * limitations under the License.
016 */
017package org.apache.juneau.commons.utils;
018
019import java.util.concurrent.*;
020import java.util.function.*;
021
022/**
023 * Utility methods for composing {@link Predicate} instances.
024 */
025@SuppressWarnings({
026   "java:S106" // System.err usage is intentional for debug peek() methods
027})
028public final class PredicateUtils {
029
030   /**
031    * Consumes the specified value if the predicate is <jk>null</jk> or matches the specified value.
032    *
033    * @param <T> The type being consumed.
034    * @param predicate The predicate.
035    * @param consumer The consumer.
036    * @param value The value.
037    */
038   public static <T> void consumeIf(Predicate<T> predicate, Consumer<T> consumer, T value) {
039      if (test(predicate, value))
040         consumer.accept(value);
041   }
042
043   /**
044    * Returns a function that prints the input value to stderr and returns it unchanged.
045    *
046    * <p>
047    * Useful for debugging streams by inserting into a stream pipeline with {@code .map(peek())}.
048    *
049    * <h5 class='section'>Example:</h5>
050    * <p class='bjava'>
051    *    list.stream()
052    *       .map(peek())
053    *       .filter(x -&gt; x != <jk>null</jk>)
054    *       .collect(Collectors.toList());
055    * </p>
056    *
057    * @param <T> The type of value.
058    * @return A function that prints and returns the value.
059    */
060   public static <T> Function<T,T> peek() {
061      return v -> {
062         System.err.println(v);
063         return v;
064      };
065   }
066
067   /**
068    * Returns a function that prints the input value to stderr using a custom formatter and returns it unchanged.
069    *
070    * <p>
071    * Useful for debugging streams by inserting into a stream pipeline with {@code .map(peek(...))}.
072    *
073    * <h5 class='section'>Example:</h5>
074    * <p class='bjava'>
075    *    list.stream()
076    *       .map(peek(<js>"Processing: {0}"</js>, x -&gt; x.getName()))
077    *       .filter(x -&gt; x != <jk>null</jk>)
078    *       .collect(Collectors.toList());
079    * </p>
080    *
081    * @param <T> The type of value.
082    * @param message A format string using {@code {0}} as placeholder for the formatted value.
083    * @param formatter A function to extract/format the value for display.
084    * @return A function that prints and returns the value.
085    */
086   public static <T> Function<T,T> peek(String message, Function<T,?> formatter) {
087      return v -> {
088         System.err.println(message.replace("{0}", String.valueOf(formatter.apply(v))));
089         return v;
090      };
091   }
092
093   /**
094    * Returns <jk>true</jk> if the specified predicate is <jk>null</jk> or matches the specified value.
095
096    * @param <T> The type being tested.
097    * @param predicate The predicate.
098    * @param value The value to test.
099    * @return <jk>true</jk> if the specified predicate is <jk>null</jk> or matches the specified value.
100    */
101   public static <T> boolean test(Predicate<T> predicate, T value) {
102      return (predicate == null || predicate.test(value));
103   }
104
105   /**
106    * Returns a predicate that filters out duplicate elements based on a key extractor function.
107    *
108    * <p>
109    * This is useful for filtering streams to keep only the first occurrence of each unique key.
110    *
111    * <h5 class='section'>Example:</h5>
112    * <p class='bjava'>
113    *    <jc>// Filter a stream to keep only methods with unique signatures</jc>
114    *    Stream&lt;Method&gt; <jv>methods</jv> = ...;
115    *    Stream&lt;Method&gt; <jv>unique</jv> = <jv>methods</jv>
116    *       .filter(PredicateUtils.<jsm>distinctByKey</jsm>(Method::<jsm>getName</jsm>));
117    * </p>
118    *
119    * @param <T> The element type.
120    * @param keyExtractor A function that extracts the key from each element.
121    * @return A predicate that returns <jk>true</jk> for the first occurrence of each unique key,
122    *    and <jk>false</jk> for subsequent occurrences with the same key.
123    */
124   public static <T> Predicate<T> distinctByKey(Function<? super T, ?> keyExtractor) {
125      var seen = ConcurrentHashMap.newKeySet();
126      return t -> seen.add(keyExtractor.apply(t));
127   }
128
129   private PredicateUtils() {}
130}
